distinctive_concerns.blind_spots: Fewer than 5 topics meet the ≥2-turn threshold across the sample (only 1 topic qualifies). No turns on fuel hedging, fuel cost exposure, booking curve shape/cancellation trends, or fleet capacity/schedule cadence in the available history (n_turns = 0 for these items across 5 turns in 2022-Q4).
distinctive_concerns.stance_drift: n_calls = 1; insufficient to characterise multi-quarter drift. Only 2022-Q4 present, so no quarter-pair changes can be reliably cited.

coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Title listed as Managing Director and Senior Analyst (explicit). Multiple back-and-forth turns on the 2022-Q4 call where management answers him directly suggest established coverage (2022-Q4).
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Covers cruise / leisure / travel with a focus on booking channels, yields, and cost guidance within the cruise operator context (2022-Q4).
rhetorical_signature: Opens with "Hi. Good morning, everyone." in 1 of 1 calls (100%)
Sometimes closes with a brief signoff: "Okay. Thank you for the color. I'm all set." (2022-Q4)
Tone: concise, polite, clarification-seeking; no confrontational language observed (2022-Q4)

reasoning_style.primary_mode_justification: Asks both modeling/benchmark clarifications (quantitative) and channel/destination behavior (qualitative) on 2022-Q4 (e.g., apples-to-apples guidance question and direct-booking destination question).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Typically asks single focused questions but will follow up for depth; 1 of 5 turns contains ≥2 question sentences (~20%). He took a direct follow-up to drill deeper on the same topic (2022-Q4, turn 4).
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: Prefers explicit baselines and wording (asked whether percentage guidances were "apples-to-apples" vs 2019 in 2022-Q4). Count of turns referencing '2019' baseline = 1. Count of turns using 'bps' = 0.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: Anchors to the 2019 baseline and to management's just-delivered wording (asked about "percentage guidances" and "versus 2019" in 2022-Q4). No evidence of anchoring to peers in this history; anchoring examples are from 2022-Q4.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #8–#9 on the 2022-Q4 call (was #8 on the first listed appearance and #9 on subsequent turns within the same call).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early to speak he opens with modelling/clarification questions about guidance baselines (asked "are those apples-to-apples based numbers?" on 2022-Q4).

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Evidence: followed initial direct-booking question with a deeper pre‑COVID acceleration follow-up, quoting "have you seen a greater acceleration" (2022-Q4).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.stay_on_topic_anyway: Evidence: pressed the distribution/channel topic twice in the same call, quoting "do you see that disproportionately going" (2022-Q4).

explicit_peer_references.examples: no evidence in history — 0 turns explicitly cite another analyst's question in the provided transcript (2022-Q4).

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: Takes at least one follow-up turn on the same topic for deeper drilling (took a follow-up to the direct-booking question once in 2022-Q4 to ask about pre‑COVID acceleration).

framing_overrides_by_theme.yield_decomposition: Verifies apples-to-apples versus 2019 reported baseline before accepting bps figures.
